Deployment

Docker

The simplest way to run PicFast:

docker run -d -p 8080:8080 \
  -v $(pwd)/data:/data \
  -e PICFAST_DATABASE_URL=postgres://user:pass@host:5432/picfast \
  -e PICFAST_JWT_SECRET=your-secret \
  picfast/picfast

Docker Compose

Recommended for production. See the Quick Start for a full example.

Environment Variables

All config keys can be set via environment variables using the PICFAST_ prefix with . replaced by _:

PICFAST_SERVER_PORT=8080
PICFAST_SERVER_BASE_URL=https://pics.example.com
PICFAST_JWT_SECRET=your-strong-secret
PICFAST_DATABASE_URL=postgres://picfast:picfast@localhost:5432/picfast
PICFAST_STORAGE_LOCAL_ROOT=/data/uploads
PICFAST_STORAGE_THUMBNAIL_DIR=/data/thumbnails

Reverse Proxy

Nginx

server {n    listen 443 ssl;n    server_name pics.example.com;nn    ssl_certificate /etc/ssl/certs/pics.crt;n    ssl_certificate_key /etc/ssl/private/pics.key;nn    client_max_body_size 100M;nn    location / {n        proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:8080;n        proxy_set_header Host $host;n        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;n        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;n        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;n    }
}

Traefik

Example labels for Docker Compose:

labels:
  - "traefik.enable=true"
  - "traefik.http.routers.picfast.rule=Host(`pics.example.com`)"
  - "traefik.http.routers.picfast.tls=true"
  - "traefik.http.services.picfast.loadbalancer.server.port=8080"

Database Backup

Use pg_dump to back up the database:

pg_dump -U picfast -h localhost picfast > backup.sql

Or in Docker:

docker exec -t postgres pg_dump -U picfast picfast > backup.sql

Updating

docker pull picfast/picfast:latest
docker compose down
docker compose up -d

Migrations run automatically on startup.

PicFast

Open source, self-hosted image hosting
